comments.author: @Outland_US
comments.text: Honestly, seeing how far $1 goes in the Czech Republic compared to inflation here in the US is wild. But what surprised you more: the beer prices, or how a country this modern became one of the most atheist places on earth? Hype and drop your thoughts below!

comments.author: @NICEGLOBE05
comments.text: Czech women are exceptionally beautiful, but what stands out most is their sharp intellect, independence, and dry sense of humor. This documentary perfectly captures the balance between Prague’s vibrant, modern lifestyle and the timeless charm of old Bohemia. 10/10 travel journalism!

replies.author: @Outland_US
replies.text: Thank you for watching. Considering its unique culture, $1 beers, and low living costs, do you think the Czech Republic is the best European country for retirement planning, or does another destination beat it?

comments.author: @jeffnotnuts8205
comments.text: Numbers are not 100% valid.  Transport tickets:  NOT 20 kc, 39 kc for 30 minutes.  Using App, 36 kc.  other option 90 min, 24 hours, 72 hours.  $1 beer (21 kc), for 1/2 l, nope!  OLD news.  Try, $2 to $2.50 and clubs higher.  Maybe supermarket beer prices (on sale).  So, prices are questionable, but still cheap.

comments.author: @janfrosty3392
comments.text: Some footage  in this shitty 'report' does net makes sense. Over all lots of BS.

comments.author: @FredWesterman
comments.text: I'm watching from Baltimore MD USA at 2:58pm

replies.author: @Outland_US
replies.text: Thanks for watching from Baltimore! With $1 beers and low costs, many Americans actually consider the Czech Republic for their retirement planning. Would you ever trade Maryland for a peaceful life in Prague?

comments.author: @AmazingGlobalDiscoveries
comments.text: 91% atheist, $1 beers, and a 99% literacy rate. Czechs are literally playing life on a completely different level. Great video.

replies.author: @Outland_US
replies.text: Thank you !!! That really made my day! Those specific statistics shape the daily reality and structure of the country. The combination of high education rates and affordable daily expenses like the $1 beer makes it an intriguing option for global retirement planning.

comments.author: @JimAllen-h8o
comments.text: It sounds like a great place to retire. I wish he would have said what the rate of money exchange was to the dollar amount..!! But it really looks like a very BEAUTIFUL PLACE to live in. But they probably don’t want AMERICANS PEOPLE living there. Maybe I am wrong..!!

replies.author: @TravelToFacts
replies.text: If you had the chance, would you choose to retire there?

replies.author: @Outland_US
replies.text: Yes, absolutely! The train system here is fantastic and connects seamlessly to many neighboring European countries. Having such easy access to international travel is a huge bonus to consider for your retirement planning. Let me know if you need more tips!

replies.author: @JimAllen-h8o
replies.text:
I think you for the reply so fast. I’m glad that you let me know the rate of the money exchange. It does sound like a great place to retire in and it sounds like the people are very nice. You be careful and stay safe and keep up the good work. 
GOD BLESS..!!
PS. 
Could you catch a train from there and go to any other Country, or the trains are just in this country…??

replies.author: @Outland_US
replies.text: 19,000 CZK equals roughly $800 USD. The country has a large, active expat community, including Americans. The balance of cheap public transit and expensive housing is a primary factor to weigh for your retirement planning here.
